mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative 20-day forward returns do not occur in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% represents restrictive monetary policy that increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% currently below trigger threshold
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations, which translates to negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices earnings forecasts.
failure_condition: When RSAFS declines > 2% MoM, SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 52% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) jointly signal real economic capacity utilization. When both decline simultaneously, it indicates demand destruction exceeding supply adjustment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 1% and PAYEMS declines > 50k in same month
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and PAYEMS decline as specified, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 58% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) jointly capture construction sector momentum and aggregate demand. When both accelerate, forward equity returns are positive over 60 trading days due to multiplier effects on employment and corporate profits.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% and GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 974.0 thousand units, GDPC1 at 18977.363 billion
failure_condition: When both HOUST and GDPC1 meet acceleration thresholds, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 55% of instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 3.5% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals real rate tightening that erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: stagflation or high-inflation monetary tightening regime
failure_condition: When both CPI and FEDFUNDS meet thresholds, SPX produces negative 20-day returns in < 54% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth above 150k per month sustained over 3 months signals robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When 3-month PAYEMS growth > 150k, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 56% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline below 97.0 index level signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 97.0 or declines > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: When INDPRO meets decline threshold, SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 51% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
